content:
Given the undirected dynamic graph with the edges:
[(2, 5, 3), (2, 6, 3), (4, 7, 3), (3, 8, 8), (4, 5, 8), (2, 7, 10), (1, 2, 11), (1, 5, 12), (5, 8, 12), (7, 9, 12), (6, 7, 14), (0, 2, 15), (5, 6, 18)],

We need to find the nodes linked with node 7 exactly at time 10.

First, we will scan each edge (u, v, t) and collect the other node whenever the time t matches the queried time AND one of the nodes matches the queried node.

For time 10:

* Edge (2, 5, 3) has node 2 and time 10. -> Add node 5.
* Edge (2, 6, 3) has node 2 and time 10. -> Add node 6.
* Edge (4, 7, 3) has node 4 and time 10. -> Add node 7.
* Edge (3, 8, 8) has node 3 and time 8. -> Ignore.
* Edge (4, 5, 8) has node 4 and time 8. -> Ignore.
* Edge (2, 7, 10) has node 2 and time 10. -> Ignore.
* Edge (1, 2, 11) has node 1 and time 11. -> Ignore.
* Edge (1, 5, 12) has node 1 and time 12. -> Ignore.
* Edge (5, 8, 12) has node 5 and time 12. -> Ignore.
* Edge (7, 9, 12) has node 7 and time 12. -> Ignore.
* Edge (6, 7, 14) has node 6 and time 14. -> Ignore.
* Edge (0, 2, 15) has node 0 and time 15. -> Ignore.
* Edge (5, 6, 18) has node 5 and time 18. -> Ignore.

The nodes matched are 5 and 7.

Answer: [5, 7]
